tools_used: provisioned snapshot and filed-document text (petition, questions-presented)
helpful: Full petition text provisioned on disk made the split/vehicle analysis possible without any network retrieval.
Per-Term paid vs IFP grant rates in statpack.json gave a much better anchor than the whole-docket rate.
The snapshot's ProceedingsandOrder list cleanly exposed the decisive CFR-after-waiver signal.
gaps: The 'Segment base rate by salience band' table the predict prompt tells us to prefer is not present in the committed metrics/statpack.md.

fedcourts query by --citation returned zero rows for Nollan/Dolan/Koontz/Sheetz; cert-petition priors don't appear to be citation-indexed, so no conditional grant rate for CFR'd petitions was retrievable (a 'grant rate given call-for-response' cut in the statpack would have been the single most useful number for this cell).
No brief-in-opposition exists yet (response due 2026-08-03); a scheduled re-run after the BIO lands would let the prediction absorb the respondent's vehicle arguments.
notes: Two corpus queries each reported ranged corpus reads: 420 GET(s), 110100480 byte(s) for zero rows; citation-overlap retrieval seems expensive relative to its yield on SCOTUS cert cells.
